title: Which data is a bad fit for caching?
prompt_md: Which of these is the **worst** fit for caching?
options: Data that must always be perfectly up-to-date and changes on nearly every request, such as a live account balance during a transaction
A product catalog page that changes a few times per day
A list of country codes that almost never changes
An expensive analytics report that's identical for all users for an hour
explanation_md: Caching trades freshness for speed, so it shines on data that is read often and changes rarely (country codes, catalog pages, hourly reports). Data that must be **perfectly current** and changes on nearly every request — like a live balance during a payment — is a poor fit: any staleness can be wrong or even dangerous, and a value that changes every request gets a near-zero hit rate anyway, so the cache adds risk without saving work.
